service_brief: Ascendly is a full-stack revenue marketing agency that helps B2B companies close more clients by building and operating end-to-end revenue systems. Ascendly handles the entire revenue pipeline from prospect identification, list building, outbound campaigns, LinkedIn outreach, cold calling coordination, lead enrichment, AI-personalized messaging, buying-signal tracking, website visitor identification, omnichannel retargeting, automated follow-up, CRM routing, and pipeline reporting into one managed system.
main_offer: Ascendly builds and runs done-for-you revenue systems that combine outbound prospecting, inbound lead capture, cold email, LinkedIn outreach, phone outreach, prospect enrichment, intent signal tracking, website visitor identification, cold email retargeting via paid ads, multi-channel follow-up automation, and pipeline visibility into one compounding system that generates and closes more clients predictably.
what_we_are_pitching: We are pitching Ascendly as a done-for-you revenue marketing partner for B2B companies that sell high-value products or services, have long or consultative sales cycles, and need a reliable, scalable system to generate qualified conversations and close more contracts without adding internal headcount.
target_outcome: Ascendly helps clients close more clients, reduce reliance on referrals or inbound-only demand, keep prospects warm and engaged across multiple channels, eliminate pipeline gaps, and build a predictable path to revenue growth.
problems_we_solve: No consistent flow of qualified new business conversations
Over-reliance on referrals, word of mouth, or inbound-only pipeline
Deals going cold because no systematic follow-up exists after first contact
Sales team spending time on unqualified or low-fit prospects
Pipeline is unpredictable and revenue is hard to forecast
Good leads visiting the website but never identified or followed up with
Outreach going to the wrong people with the wrong message
No visibility into where deals are stalling or dropping off
Marketing and sales operating in silos with no connected system
Company expanding into new markets or verticals with no established pipeline
Wasted ad spend on audiences that have never shown any intent
No multi-channel presence meaning prospects are only touched once and forgotten
Founder or leadership spending too much time on prospecting instead of closing
No process for re-engaging stalled or lost opportunities

pipeline_and_prospecting: Inconsistent or unpredictable pipeline month to month
No repeatable outbound system that generates qualified conversations
Relying entirely on referrals with no control over when new business arrives
Difficulty identifying and reaching the right decision-makers at target companies
Prospecting lists that are inaccurate, unverified, or poorly targeted
No way to identify which website visitors are actually potential buyers
outreach_and_messaging: Cold outreach that sounds generic and gets ignored or marked as spam
Messaging that leads with features and process instead of outcomes and results
Outreach that lacks personalization and fails to connect with the prospect's actual situation
No multi-channel outreach strategy meaning prospects are only contacted once
Email deliverability issues that prevent messages from reaching inboxes
No follow-up sequence after an initial email or LinkedIn message
conversion_and_follow_up: Deals going cold after initial interest because no structured follow-up exists
Meetings booked but not converting because prospects are not properly warmed up
No system to re-engage prospects who showed interest but did not move forward
Long sales cycles where prospects lose momentum between touches
No automated process to keep warm prospects engaged across weeks or months
Opportunities being lost simply because nobody followed up at the right time
visibility_and_reporting: No clear view of where deals are in the pipeline at any given time
Unable to track which outreach activities are generating real revenue
No data connecting marketing activity to closed contracts
Leadership cannot forecast revenue because pipeline is too unpredictable
No way to identify which prospects are showing buying intent signals
growth_and_scale: Expanding into new markets or verticals with no pipeline infrastructure in place
Founder or sales leader doing all prospecting manually with no scalable process
Hiring more salespeople but having no system to feed them qualified conversations
Losing deals to competitors who are more visible and present across multiple channels
Paying for ads but targeting audiences who have never shown any intent or interest

universal_outcomes: Consistent flow of qualified sales conversations with decision-makers
Predictable pipeline that makes revenue forecasting possible
Reduced dependence on referrals and inbound-only demand
More closed contracts without adding internal sales or marketing headcount
Decision-makers warmed up and educated before the first sales conversation
No deal going cold due to lack of follow-up
Full visibility into pipeline performance and where revenue is coming from

b2b_saas_and_technology: Consistent demo pipeline with qualified buyers who match the ICP
Shorter sales cycles because prospects are educated and warmed up before the call
Expansion into new verticals or geographies with outbound infrastructure already built
it_services_and_consulting: Steady flow of qualified project conversations without relying on referrals
More retainer or long-term contract opportunities surfaced through systematic outreach
Decision-makers at target accounts identified and engaged before competitors reach them
professional_and_business_services: Predictable new client flow replacing inconsistent referral-based growth
Higher quality conversations because outreach is specific to the prospect's situation
Faster growth into new industries or regions through targeted outbound campaigns
industrial_and_traditional_industries: More qualified project inquiries from commercial buyers and procurement contacts
Pipeline built in new geographies or verticals without a local sales presence
Long-cycle deals kept warm and progressed systematically until a decision is made
healthcare_and_life_sciences: Qualified conversations with hospital administrators, practice managers, or procurement leaders
Compliant outreach that builds trust with risk-averse buyers over time
Predictable pipeline for high-value contracts in a complex buying environment

what_we_do.summary: Ascendly operates a full-stack revenue system across outbound, inbound, and multi-channel follow-up. Every component is designed to generate, warm, and close qualified pipeline.

capabilities.name: Outbound Cold Email
capabilities.description: Hyper-personalized cold email campaigns sent at scale using verified, enriched prospect lists and AI-assisted personalization. Built for deliverability and reply rates, not just volume.

capabilities.name: LinkedIn Outreach
capabilities.description: Targeted LinkedIn connection and messaging sequences to reach decision-makers who do not respond to email, running in parallel with cold email for multi-channel coverage.

capabilities.name: Cold Calling Coordination
capabilities.description: Outbound phone outreach coordinated alongside email and LinkedIn to increase contact rates and surface warm opportunities faster.

capabilities.name: Prospect Identification & List Building
capabilities.description: ICP-based prospecting using intent signals, buying triggers, job change alerts, hiring signals, and tech stack data to identify the right companies and contacts before any outreach begins.

capabilities.name: Lead Enrichment & Verification
capabilities.description: Every prospect is enriched with verified contact data, company signals, and contextual information before outreach so messaging is accurate, relevant, and personalized.

capabilities.name: Website Visitor Identification
capabilities.description: Identifying companies and decision-makers visiting the client's website even if they never fill out a form, then routing them into outreach or follow-up sequences automatically.

capabilities.name: Buying Signal & Intent Tracking
capabilities.description: Monitoring job change triggers, hiring signals, tech stack changes, and other intent indicators to time outreach when a prospect is most likely to buy.

capabilities.name: Cold Email Retargeting via Paid Ads
capabilities.description: Uploading only the prospects who clicked links in cold emails into LinkedIn Matched Audiences or Meta Custom Audiences so ad spend targets only people who already showed interest, not cold audiences.

capabilities.name: Multi-Channel Follow-Up Automation
capabilities.description: Automated follow-up sequences across email, LinkedIn, and other channels so no prospect goes cold after initial contact. Every touch is timed and contextual.

capabilities.name: Inbound Lead Capture & Routing
capabilities.description: Capturing every inbound lead from website forms, ad clicks, and other sources, enriching them automatically, qualifying them against the ICP, and routing them to the right person or sequence instantly.

capabilities.name: Deal Re-Engagement
capabilities.description: Systematically re-engaging stalled or cold deals in the pipeline with new angles, updated messaging, and fresh touchpoints so opportunities are worked until there is a clear outcome.

capabilities.name: CRM Integration & Pipeline Routing
capabilities.description: All leads, prospects, and deal activity synced and routed through the client's CRM so nothing falls through the cracks and the sales team always knows what to do next.

capabilities.name: Pipeline Visibility & Reporting
capabilities.description: Real-time reporting on pipeline created, deals progressed, meetings booked, and revenue generated so clients always know what the system is producing and where to focus.

icp_summary: Ascendly targets B2B companies with high-value products or services, long or consultative sales cycles, and a clear need for predictable pipeline. Ideal clients sell contracts worth $10K to $1M+ and need consistent qualified conversations with decision-makers such as founders, executives, VPs, and Directors.

icp.primary_fit: B2B companies with high-value products or services, long or consultative sales cycles, strong customer lifetime value, and a need for consistent qualified meetings and closed contracts.
company_characteristics: Products or services worth $10K to $1M+ per contract
Dedicated sales team or founder-led sales motion
Need for qualified conversations with decision-makers
Selling into mid-market, commercial, or enterprise accounts
Expanding into new markets, verticals, or geographies
Looking for predictable outbound pipeline instead of relying only on referrals or inbound
No pricing listed publicly, indicating a consultative or custom sales process
Clear B2B positioning with identifiable target buyers

technology_and_saas: HR tech and workforce management platforms
EV infrastructure and energy technology companies
PropTech and real estate technology platforms
EdTech platforms serving institutions or enterprises
industrial_and_traditional_industries: Commercial cleaning and facilities companies with B2B contracts
financial_services: Wealth management firms targeting institutions or businesses
Business lending and alternative finance companies
Fractional CFO and financial consulting firms

non_icp.summary: Non-ICP companies are businesses where outbound pipeline generation is unlikely to produce meaningful revenue because the offer is low-ticket, consumer-focused, nonprofit, publicly priced, local-only, or does not involve a consultative sales process with identifiable decision-makers.
non_fit_categories: Nonprofit organizations and charities
Government agencies and public sector bodies
Educational institutions such as schools and universities
Restaurants, cafes, and food and beverage businesses
Salons, spas, barbershops, and beauty services
Gyms, fitness studios, and wellness centers
Small local consumer service businesses
Low-ticket ecommerce stores selling to consumers
Retail stores and brick-and-mortar consumer businesses
Freelancers and solo operators with no team or scalable offer
Creator businesses, influencers, and personal brand businesses
Affiliate marketing sites and content publishers
Hobby businesses and passion projects
Companies with publicly listed low-ticket pricing (under $3K per contract)
Subscription consumer apps with self-serve pricing pages
Companies selling exclusively to individual consumers with no B2B component
Real estate agents operating as individuals rather than commercial firms
Companies with no clear service, product, or target customer
Businesses where website appears parked, inactive, or under construction
Companies that explicitly state they do not work with outside vendors or agencies
non_fit_signals: No clear B2B offer or identifiable business buyer
Publicly listed pricing under $3K per transaction or contract
Self-serve signup or checkout with no sales process
Nonprofit or charity registration or language
Mostly consumer walk-in or direct-to-consumer business model
No evidence of a sales team, sales process, or consultation step
No meaningful contract value or long-term customer relationship
Website is parked, inactive, or has no clear commercial offer
Company sells exclusively to local consumers with no scalable B2B motion
Founder appears to be a solo operator with no team and no scalable service
Company has no visible case studies, clients, or commercial positioning

icp_review_rules: Return ICP if the company is B2B or sells a high-value service, product, software, consulting, or commercial solution where qualified conversations with decision-makers could generate meaningful revenue.

Return ICP if the company fits technology, SaaS, IT services, consulting, professional services, industrial services, healthcare, financial services, legal, marketing, creative, logistics, construction, manufacturing, energy, or other high-value B2B categories.
Return ICP if the company appears to sell into mid-market, enterprise, commercial, professional, or institutional buyers.
Return ICP if the company likely has a consultative sales process, high lifetime customer value, high contract value, or a clear need for predictable pipeline.
Return ICP if the company has no public pricing listed, suggesting a consultative or custom sales motion.
Return Non-ICP if the company is a nonprofit, charity, government body, school, or university.
Return Non-ICP if the company publicly lists pricing under $3K per transaction with a self-serve or checkout model.
Return Non-ICP if the company is a restaurant, salon, gym, spa, cafe, small local consumer business, low-ticket store, creator business, affiliate site, hobby business, or unclear business.
Return Non-ICP if the company has no clear offer, no clear target customer, no visible business model, or no meaningful use for outbound appointment setting.
If the company is a local service business but sells high-value projects, commercial services, construction, roofing, legal, financial, healthcare, or B2B services, it can still be ICP.
If unsure, classify based on whether a qualified conversation with a decision-maker could realistically generate meaningful revenue for the company.
If ICPReview is Non-ICP, all writing variables should return N/A.
If ICPReview is ICP, generate all writing variables using only the website content and CSV row context.

icp_review_output.purpose: Decide whether the company is a good fit for Ascendly's done-for-you revenue system and pipeline generation service.

research_guidance.goal: Understand whether the prospect company sells a high-value product or service to business buyers and whether a predictable outbound and multi-channel revenue system would generate meaningful new contracts for them.

global_rules: Use only the prospect website content and CSV row context.
Do not invent clients, services, industries, numbers, results, funding, awards, partnerships, or claims.
Never use the em dash character.
Use & instead of and where natural.
Keep language simple, direct, specific, and practical.
Avoid generic praise like great website, impressive work, amazing services, strong online presence, innovative solutions, and professional team.
Avoid robotic words like leverage, robust, seamless, scalable, synergy, transformative, game-changing, cutting-edge, future-ready, unlock, empower, and elevate.
Do not use the prospect's first name, last name, full name, founder name, employee name, or contact name in any generated variable.
Do not start any variable with a person's name.
Personalization must be about the company, website, service, clients, portfolio, positioning, industry, or target customers, not the individual contact.
Never lead with what we do or how we do it. Always lead with the outcome or result the prospect will get.
Never use the word appointments or meetings as the primary outcome. Use clients, contracts, pipeline, revenue, or closed deals instead.
